calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion Adn Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendants Benjamin Coe, Stacy Cancilla
Off Calendar Jun-26-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Carol Brown's motion to compel deposition of defendants is GRANTED. Defendants Benjamin Coe and Stacy Cancilla each shall submit to a deposition, and produce documents as requested, at a mutually acceptable date and time, but not later than Friday, June 30, 2006. Commissioner: PAUL SLAVIT

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-26-2006 Vacated
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly for trial by jury.

Appearances:
JOSEPH SACRAMENTO, Esq. on behalf of plaintiff Carol Ann Brown
BENJAMIN COE and STACEY CANCILLA, defendants in pro per

The parties settled after being sent to Judge Pro Tem. Terms of the settlement are as follows: Plaintiff will pay defendants the sum of $5,000.00 cash by 8:00 p.m. this date of 6/26/06. Defendants to go to plaintiff counsel's office to get this cash. On June 29, 2006, defendants are to vacate the premises located at 766 Church Street, San Francisco, California by 8:00 p.m. Defendants are to provide keys to plaintiff attorney, and plaintiff will provide additional $5,000.00 to defendants. This exchange will happen at the property. Parties stipulated for entry of judgment, however judgment is stayed unless plaintiff fails to make payment or defendants fail to vacate premises as stated above. Upon 24 hours notice, party can appear ex parte along with proof of default for entry of judgment for possession. Each day defendants are in possession past the June 29th vacating, plaintiff shall deduct $200.00 each day from funds owing to defendants. There will be no further stays. Mutual releases were stated on the record. CC 1542 waiver were agreed to. Mutual stay-away of at least one block of the premises. Any intentional damages is $1,000.00 per violation. All parties agreed to the settlement.

Judge Pro Tem: Mark Petersen; Clerk: V. Mu; Reporter: JoAnn Bryce CSR #3321 (206)
